success_criteria: Review document produced with a verdict (accept / accept-with-changes / reject)
For each finding name severity (blocking / non-blocking), the field affected, and the suggested revision verbatim
Read-set completeness check — does jck have everything he needs from original/ to produce a doc the implementer can use without re-reading source?
Success-criteria verifiability check — can the leader objectively decide pass/fail at the end of the research mission?
Output-shape check — will the docs/research artifact be actionable for an sjl-led implement mission, or does the contract need additional success criteria to make jck's output implementer-ready?

Research doc produced with panel coordinates, label text per special, active/inactive colors (yellow=active, white=inactive, shadow=black), font identity (COPY_FONT XLFD and SDL2 equivalent slot), row/column layout
Each fact cited with original/<file>.c:<line>
Redraw triggers from ball.c, blocks.c, editor.c documented (when does the panel need to be redrawn during gameplay?)
Attract-mode behavior documented (RandomDrawSpecials)
Panel absolute position in the mainWindow documented (x, y from stage.c:CreateAllWindows), distinguished from the window-local coordinates used inside DrawSpecials()

Findings phrased as actionable input for an implement mission targeting src/game_render.c. Specifically name the SDL2 call sequence (sdl2_font_draw_shadow with SDL2F_FONT_COPY), the state accessor (special_system_get_labels + paddle_system_get_reverse for reverse_on), and the absolute window origin to add as offset. The implementer must be able to write game_render_specials() without re-reading original/special.c, original/paddle.c, or original/stage.c.
Review document produced with verdict (accept / accept-with-changes / reject)
For each finding name severity (blocking / non-blocking), the field affected, and the suggested verbatim revision
Testability angle explicitly addressed — is there a verification step that doesn't require running the game and looking at the screen?
Edge-case angle explicitly addressed — game_render_specials() called with NULL ctx members, unexpected paddle reverse value
Regression-risk angle explicitly addressed — what makes this fix self-defending against silent re-breakage?
Recommendations are concrete and additive (no demand to rewrite the contract from scratch)
game_render_specials() defined in src/game_render.c per the implementation sketch in docs/research section 9
Function called from game_render_frame() in the SDL2ST_GAME and SDL2ST_PAUSE branches, after game_render_messages and game_render_timer
Panel renders at absolute (292, 655) — the specialWindow origin from original/stage.c:263 — using SDL2F_FONT_COPY with shadow text
All 8 specials in correct row/column positions per special_system_get_labels() (matches original/special.c:152-222)
Active labels render in yellow ({255,255,50,255}); inactive in white ({255,255,255,255}); shadow black at offset SDL2F_SHADOW_OFFSET
game_render_specials() guards against ctx->special == NULL and ctx->paddle == NULL — returns immediately without dereferencing either
Pure helper game_render_specials_coords(int lh, const special_label_info_t *labels, int i, int *abs_x, int *abs_y) extracted to keep coordinate math testable without an SDL2 context

tests/test_game_render_specials.c added — CMocka tests verifying that game_render_specials_coords() returns the correct abs_x, abs_y for each of the 8 labels given known lh, verified against the constants in include/special_system.h without any SDL2 context
Test asserts label 0 ("Reverse") maps to abs_x=297, abs_y=658 (SPECIAL_PANEL_ORIGIN_X+SPECIAL_COL0_X=292+5, SPECIAL_PANEL_ORIGIN_Y+SPECIAL_ROW0_Y=655+3) for lh=15
Test asserts active labels produce yellow {255,255,50,255}, inactive labels produce white {255,255,255,255} (color-selection branch)
Test asserts game_render_specials() with ctx->special == NULL and ctx->paddle == NULL does not crash; ASan build also passes this case
tests/test_game_render_specials.c registered as a ctest target via tests/CMakeLists.txt
make check passes (format, cppcheck, markdownlint, ctest debug, ctest asan, dpkg-buildpackage, lintian)
